title: PMR-446 voice-radio coverage for ranger patrols out of a base camp
summary: Compute the voice-radio coverage heatmap for ranger handhelds across a 25 km × 18 km patrol AOI, given a base-camp repeater on a 12 m mast. The link is `generic` (no specialized link-type plugin) — it's an FM voice channel; the engine's universal link-budget output plus a binary above-sensitivity raster is exactly what the field commander needs.
demonstrates: generic link_type — no plugin extension; universal link-budget output (§4.6, §6.1)
Plain geotiff + contours; no link-type semantic outputs

sites.name: ranger-camp-shingwedzi-mast
sites.notes: 12 m steel mast at base camp.

notes: `pmr-446-handheld` is an Equipment Profile pairing the EU PMR-446 voice radio (`pmr-446-446mhz`) with the 446 MHz whip antenna. `link_type: generic`, so stage 10 of the pipeline is a no-op — only the universal `geotiff` / `stats` / `link_budget` outputs apply. KMZ is the operationally useful artifact; a field commander loads it on a tablet running Google Earth offline. Contour levels are in dBm, ordered as the operator wants the legend.
